prompt:
Our CLI snapshot harness stores each expected terminal line as ONE escaped line in a fixture file, and the fixture linter needs to underline mistakes precisely, so 'somewhere in this line' errors are useless to us. I need the decoding half written properly: crate rs-unescape, src/lib.rs, stdlib only. The acceptance tests are already in tests/unescape.rs — that file, Cargo.toml and .gitignore are fixed; cargo test --offline green is the finish line.

API:
- pub fn unescape(src: &str) -> Result<String, EscapeError>
- pub struct EscapeError { pub kind: EscapeErrorKind, pub start: usize, pub end: usize } — start/end are BYTE offsets into src (half-open, start at the backslash), and they must survive multi-byte characters earlier in the line. Derives Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq; implements Display as exactly `invalid escape at bytes {start}..{end}` and std::error::Error.
- pub enum EscapeErrorKind, same derives, with these variants: TrailingBackslash, UnknownEscape(char), BareUnicode, UnterminatedUnicode, EmptyUnicode, BadHexDigit(char), OverlongUnicode, OutOfRange(u32), LoneSurrogate(u32), TruncatedByte, ByteOutOfRange(u8).

Grammar (scan left to right, first error wins, everything not after a backslash passes through verbatim):
- \n \t \r \0 \\ \" \' decode to the obvious characters.
- \x followed by exactly two hex digits (either case) is an ASCII byte escape: value above 0x7F is ByteOutOfRange(value) — higher code points must be written with the brace form. Input ending before both digits arrive is TruncatedByte (end = src.len()); a non-hex character where a digit belongs is BadHexDigit(that char) with end just past it.
- \u must be followed by '{': otherwise (including \u at end of input) it's BareUnicode with span covering just the two characters \u. Inside the braces: 1..=6 hex digits (either case), then '}'. Zero digits is EmptyUnicode (span through the '}'); a seventh hex digit is OverlongUnicode ending just past that seventh digit; a non-hex non-brace character is BadHexDigit ending just past it; end of input before '}' is UnterminatedUnicode (end = src.len()). A complete \u{...} whose value exceeds 0x10FFFF is OutOfRange(value), and U+D800..=U+DFFF is LoneSurrogate(value) — both spanning the whole escape including the closing brace.
- a backslash as the last byte of input is TrailingBackslash; a backslash before any other character is UnknownEscape(that char) with end past that character (chars can be multi-byte).

Error spans are the whole point of this ticket — the tests pin kind, start and end for every failure mode, byte-exact. Where my prose and the tests disagree, the tests win.
